name: Mid-Autumn Festival
traditions: Eating mooncakes; family reunions; lantern displays; moon gazing; burning incense; lion dances; making offerings to the moon.
historicalBackground: The Mid-Autumn Festival dates to the Tang Dynasty (618–907). It celebrates the harvest and the legend of Chang'e, the Moon Goddess of immortality.
description: China's harvest moon celebration, where families reunite under the full moon to share mooncakes and celebrate togetherness.
